content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 29, 1990

Honorable Ross Johnson
Assembly Republican Leader
State Capitol, Room 2114
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA  94249-0001

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-89-716

Dear Assembly Member Johnson:

     You have requested confirmation of oral advice received from
staff concerning application of campaign contribution limits under
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

     This will confirm that your letter of December 22, 1989, ac-
curately reflects staff advice concerning the application of fis-
cal year contribution limits to contributions raised to pay off
campaign debts from special elections. Once a special election
cycle or special runoff election cycle is completed, subsequent
contributions raised to pay off campaign debts from that special
election count toward the regular fiscal year contribution limits.

     Section 85305(b) defines the special election cycles as fol-
lows:

          (1)  "Special election cycle" means the day on
     which the office becomes vacant until the day of
     the special election.


___________

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-89-716
Page 2


(2) "Special runoff election cycle" means the
day after the special election until the day of the
special runoff election.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, you
may contact me at (916) 332-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Margaret W. Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:MWE:aa

January 8, 1990


Honorable Ross Johnson
Member of the Assembly
State Capitol
P.O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA  94249-0001

                                        Re:  Letter No. 89-716

Dear Assemblyman Johnson:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on December 28, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission.  If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Margaret Ellison an attorney in the Legal
Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice.  If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed.  If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can.  (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

KED:plh


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

SACRAMENTO OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
P.O. BOX 942849
SACRAMENTO, CA 94249-0001
(916) 445-7448

DISTRICT OFFICE
1501 NORTH HARBOR BOULEVARD
SUITE 201
FULLERTON, CA 92635
(714) 738-5853
(213) 697-0603

Assembly
California Legislature

ROSS JOHNSON
ASSEMBLYMAN, SIXTY-FOURTH DISTRICT

ASSEMBLY REPUBLICAN LEADER

December 22, 1989


Mr. John Larson
Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Suite 800
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear John:

I would appreciate receiving confirmation for oral advice from your staff concerning debts from special elections.

It is my understanding that it is your position that once a special election cycle or special runoff election cycle is completed, a candidate in that special election must raise contributions to pay off any campaign debts within the regular fiscal year limitations of Prop. 73. Under such an interpretation, any contributions raised to pay off campaign debts from special elections would be cumulated with contributions raised for any other campaign accounts of the candidate.

As defined in Section 85305, special election cycle "means the day on which the office becomes vacant until the day of the special election." Special runoff election cycle "means the day after the special election until the day of the special runoff election."

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

ROSS JOHNSON
Assembly Republican Leader

sections.question: It is my understanding that it is your position that once a special election cycle or special runoff election cycle is completed, a candidate in that special election must raise contributions to payoff any campaign debts within the regular fiscal year limitations of Prop. 73. Under such an interpretation, any contributions raised to payoff campaign debts from special elections would be cumulated with contributions raised for any other campaign accounts of the candidate.
sections.conclusion: Once a special election cycle or special runoff election cycle is completed, subsequent contributions raised to payoff campaign debts from that special election count toward the regular fiscal year contribution limits.
sections.question_synthetic: Do contributions raised to pay off campaign debts from special elections count toward regular fiscal year contribution limits under Proposition 73 once the special election cycle is completed?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that contributions raised to pay off campaign debts from special elections count toward the regular fiscal year contribution limits after the special election cycle ends.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of three parts: (1) incoming request from Assembly Member Johnson dated 12/22/1989, (2) FPPC acknowledgment letter dated 1/8/1990, and (3) FPPC formal response dated 1/29/1990. The response confirms prior oral advice. Question and conclusion extracted from the formal response letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: It is my understanding that it is your position that once a special election cycle or special runoff election cycle is completed, a candidate in that special election must raise contributions to payoff any campaign debts within the regular fiscal year limitations of Prop. 73. Under such an interpretation, any contributions raised to payoff campaign debts from special elections would be cumulated with contributions raised for any other campaign accounts of the candidate.

CONCLUSION: Once a special election cycle or special runoff election cycle is completed, subsequent contributions raised to payoff campaign debts from that special election count toward the regular fiscal year contribution limits.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms oral advice to Assembly Republican Leader Ross Johnson that contributions raised to pay off campaign debts from special elections are subject to regular fiscal year contribution limits once the special election cycle is completed.
